ArtifactStatus.description: Whether an expected artifact is present or missing.
ArtifactStatus.title: ArtifactStatus

ConsumerType.description: Who or what may consume an artifact.
ConsumerType.title: ConsumerType

ExcludedPath.description:
A path excluded from the artifact manifest because it is infrastructure noise.

Examples: coverage.ini, .coverage.*, sitecustomize.py, __pycache__/

path.description: Path as it appears in the bucket.
path.title: Path

pattern.description: Glob pattern that matched, if pattern-based exclusion was used.
pattern.title: Pattern

ExcludedPath.title: ExcludedPath

Limitation.description: Known limitations or caveats on an artifact or manifest.
Limitation.title: Limitation

Location.description:
Path-layout class for an artifact.

Describes WHERE in the file system the artifact lives relative
to the per-run bucket, not what the artifact IS.

Do not collapse REPO_SINGLETON into per-run locations —
architecture-invariant outputs have no per-run scope.
Location.title: Location

ManagedArtifactEntry.description:
A single artifact entry in the artifact manifest.

Generic fields apply to any managed repo.
artifact_kind and source_stage accept str so producer profiles
can use their own vocabulary without constraining the generic model.

artifact_id.description: Stable, unique identifier for this artifact within the run. Suggested format: '{producer}:{audit_type}:{source_stage}:{filename_slug}'. Must be stable across manifest rewrites during the same run.
artifact_id.title: Artifact Id

artifact_kind.description: Producer-vocabulary artifact kind (e.g. VideoFoundryArtifactKind values). Use 'unknown' when the kind cannot be determined.
artifact_kind.title: Artifact Kind

path.description: Path as recorded by the producer. May be absolute or relative.
path.title: Path

relative_path.description: Path relative to run_root or artifact_root, where applicable. None for repo_singleton artifacts where no single root applies.
relative_path.title: Relative Path

path_role.description: Semantic role of this artifact path.

source_stage.description: Producer stage that created this artifact. Use producer profile vocabulary (e.g. VideoFoundrySourceStage). None for lifecycle or unknown sources.
source_stage.title: Source Stage

status.description: Whether the artifact is present, missing, or expected.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the artifact was first written.
created_at.title: Created At

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of the most recent write.
updated_at.title: Updated At

size_bytes.description: File size in bytes at last observation. None if not yet measured.
size_bytes.title: Size Bytes

consumer_types.description: Who or what is expected to consume this artifact.
consumer_types.title: Consumer Types

valid_for.description: Temporal/contextual scope for which this artifact is meaningful.
valid_for.title: Valid For

limitations.description: Known caveats on this artifact's completeness or reliability.
limitations.title: Limitations

description.description: Human-readable description of what this artifact contains.
description.title: Description

metadata.description: Producer-specific metadata not covered by generic fields.
metadata.title: Metadata

ManagedArtifactEntry.title: ManagedArtifactEntry

ManifestStatus.description:
Lifecycle status of the artifact manifest itself.

Distinct from RunStatus — the manifest can be partial even when
the run has completed (e.g. some artifact writes failed).
ManifestStatus.title: ManifestStatus

PathRole.description: Semantic role of an artifact path.
PathRole.title: PathRole

RunStatus.description:
Lifecycle status of a managed audit run.

Canonical values for run_status.json and artifact manifests.

Decision: VideoFoundry currently emits "in_progress" (legacy).
The contract canonicalises this to RUNNING. Phase 5 must switch
VideoFoundry to emit "running". IN_PROGRESS_LEGACY is accepted
during the transition period by readers but must not be emitted
by compliant producers.
RunStatus.title: RunStatus

ValidFor.description: Temporal/contextual scope for which an artifact is meaningful.
ValidFor.title: ValidFor

description:
Schema for artifact_manifest.json as defined by the managed-repo audit contract.

Produced by VideoFoundry (Phase 5), defined by OperationsCenter (Phase 2),
read by OpsCenter indexing (Phase 7).

The manifest is written incrementally. It is valid during initializing
and running states. OpsCenter must tolerate a partial manifest.

Repo-singleton rule:
    Artifacts with location=REPO_SINGLETON have run_id=None in their
    metadata and valid_for=[latest_snapshot]. The manifest run_id still
    identifies the run that last updated the singleton reference.

contract_name.description: Identifies this as a managed-repo-audit contract file.
contract_name.title: Contract Name

repo_id.description: Stable repo identifier matching the managed repo config.
repo_id.title: Repo Id

run_id.description: UUID hex generated by OperationsCenter before invocation.
run_id.title: Run Id

manifest_status.description: Current lifecycle status of the manifest itself.

run_status.description: Current lifecycle status of the audit run.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the manifest was first created.
created_at.title: Created At

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of the most recent manifest update.
updated_at.title: Updated At

finalized_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the manifest reached a terminal state. None during initializing/running. Required on completed/failed/partial.
finalized_at.title: Finalized At

artifact_root.description: Root directory for resolving artifact paths in this manifest. Typically the VideoFoundry repo root. relative_path in each entry is relative to this root where applicable.
artifact_root.title: Artifact Root

run_root.description: The per-run bucket directory (relative to repo root). Example: tools/audit/report/representative/Connective_Contours_..._{run_id}
run_root.title: Run Root

artifacts.description: Artifact entries. Populated incrementally during the run. May be empty or partial during initializing/running states.
artifacts.title: Artifacts

excluded_paths.description: Infrastructure noise paths excluded from artifact tracking. Examples: coverage.ini, .coverage.*, sitecustomize.py.
excluded_paths.title: Excluded Paths

warnings.description: Non-fatal warnings encountered during the run or manifest writing.
warnings.title: Warnings

metadata.description: Producer-specific metadata not covered by generic fields.
metadata.title: Metadata

title: ManagedArtifactManifest
